mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce borrowing costs for corporations, boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in reduced consumer spending.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances when unemployment jumps by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates robust housing sector demand, which drives positive equity returns over a quarter as construction and related industries expand.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in housing sales and starts fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side contraction, causing short-term negative equity returns during periods of high inflation.
failure_condition: Industrial production dips below its moving average but 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index combined with a static or rising federal funds rate indicates real tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where inflation accelerates while policy rates do not decline.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls falling below zero for two consecutive months signals severe labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as recession fears dominate market pricing.
failure_condition: Consecutive negative payroll prints are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and rising unemployment indicates structural supply inefficiencies, causing short-term negative equity returns as markets price in stagflation risks.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where both industrial output and unemployment rise simultaneously.
